description: TTL session tracking: increment sessions_since_last_use at session start for every active build in build-registry.json, trigger downgrade on expiry

definition.implementation_summary: Session Start step 6 in CLAUDE.md: for each active build in build-registry.json, increment sessions_since_last_use by 1. Check expiry condition: sessions_since_last_use > ttl_sessions OR now - last_used > ttl_days. If expired → trigger downgrade per protocols/core/downgrade.md. If expiring within 2 days/sessions → warn user. build-registry.json stores per-build fields: ttl_sessions (int), ttl_days (int), sessions_since_last_use (int, starts at 0), last_used (ISO date).

build_registry_fields.sessions_since_last_use: Counter incremented at each session start (int, default 0)
build_registry_fields.last_used: ISO date of last session where this build was actively loaded/used

definition.adaptation_notes: Add step to your Session Start that reads build-registry.json, iterates active builds, increments counters. Connect to your downgrade protocol for expiry handling. Ensure build-registry.json has the required fields for each build entry.

adaptation_notes: Integrate into your Session Start procedure. Ensure build-registry.json has ttl_sessions, ttl_days, sessions_since_last_use, last_used fields per build. Connect expiry trigger to your downgrade protocol.
